query:
mutation { issueCreate(input: { title: "[spike] Chat SDK Integration - Discord/Slack CLI Interface", description: "## Overview

Integrate Vercel's Chat SDK (chat-sdk.dev) to allow reps to use Consuelo directly from Discord/Slack via slash commands and rich cards.

## The Vision: Consuelo Everywhere

Meet sales reps where they already hang (Discord for startups, Slack for enterprise) instead of requiring them to switch to the Consuelo web app.

### What Chat SDK Gives Us Out-of-the-Box
- Unified API: Write bot logic once, deploy to Discord + Slack simultaneously
- JSX Cards: Rich interactive UIs that render natively on each platform
- Event Handling: Type-safe handlers for mentions, slash commands, button clicks
- State Management: Redis/in-memory adapters for distributed state
- Streaming: Real-time AI responses (perfect for coaching)

## Phase 1: The Chat CLI (Easy Wins)

Map existing CLI commands to Discord/Slack slash commands:

/consuelo queue start [category]     -> Start dialer session
/consuelo queue pause|resume|stop    -> Session controls
/consuelo upload                      -> CSV import via file attachment
/consuelo contacts search [query]     -> Search workspace contacts
/consuelo contacts next               -> Peek next in queue
/consuelo status                      -> Your current session status
/consuelo caller-id                   -> View/set outgoing number
/consuelo coaching [call-id]          -> View AI analysis from past call
/consuelo me                         -> Your stats (calls made, conversion)

Rich Cards Examples:
- Queue status with mini leaderboard
- Contact card when you hit next (shows last call outcome + notes)
- Post-call summary with transcript snippet + coaching highlights
- Real-time call status in #sales channel

## Phase 2: The Discord Dialer (Complex)

Stretch Goal: Reps do everything from Discord voice channels - no browser tab needed.

How It Works:
1. Rep joins Discord voice channel
2. Runs /consuelo dialer
3. Bot creates a private voice channel or stays in shared #sales-floor
4. Rep clicks Call Next in a rich card
5. Behind the scenes: Discord WebRTC <-> Twilio WebRTC bridge
6. Customer talks to rep through Discord voice

### ELI5: Why This Is Hard

WebRTC vs PSTN Bridge:
- Discord uses WebRTC (internet calls, like Zoom)
- Twilio Voice SDK also uses WebRTC (but for phone calls)
- They're speaking different dialects of the same language

What bridging means:
- Building a translator that converts Discord's audio packets into Twilio's format in real-time
- Every sound has to be translated instantly as it happens
- Also need to handle: latency, audio quality, DTMF tones, hold/music

Real-world analogy:
Imagine a Zoom call where one person is on Zoom and another is on a regular phone. The Zoom server has to convert between internet video calls and telephone audio - that's the bridge. Now imagine doing that 100 times a second with no delays.

Why Not Just Use Discord Voice Directly?
- Discord voice = talking computer-to-computer (like FaceTime or Zoom)
- Phone calls = talking to actual phone numbers
- Discord cant call a phone number directly

The process:
1. Rep wants to call customer at +1-555-0123
2. Discord cant dial that number
3. So we use Twilio to dial the phone number
4. Then we bridge the Twilio call (which has the customer on the line) to the Discord voice channel
5. Now customer (on phone) talks to rep (in Discord)

Real-world analogy:
Its like having a translator on a 3-way call. The translator (Twilio) calls the customer on their phone, stays on the line, and repeats everything between you (speaking into Discord) and the customer (speaking on their phone).

The hard part:
Doing this translation instantly with no lag, no echo, and good audio quality - thats the engineering challenge.

### Simpler Hybrid Approach (Recommended)

How it works:
- Keep browser-based Twilio Voice SDK for actual calls (works today, proven reliable)
- Discord purely for UI/notifications/team coordination
- When rep hits call in Consuelo dialer, bot posts to Discord
- Team sees whos calling whom without opening Consuelo tab
- Warm transfers initiated from Discord threads

Why this is better:
- Uses battle-tested Twilio Voice SDK (no custom audio pipeline)
- Still gives reps the chat-first experience
- Much faster to build and ship
- No audio quality/complexity issues

## Phase 3: Advanced Features

Thread Persistence:
- Each command creates/replies in a thread
- /consuelo call 123 creates thread
- Subsequent commands in thread act on that call
- Perfect for warm transfers - manager joins thread, sees context, clicks take over

Voice Channel Integration:
- Bot joins sales teams voice channel
- Whispers call stats: You have 3 calls completed, 2 connects today
- Announces: @rep just qualified a $50K opportunity!

Friction-Reducing Features:
- CSV Drag-and-Drop: Drop a CSV file in Discord -> bot auto-parses
- Screenshot Receipts: When you mark a call disposition: interested, bot generates shareable win card
- Webhook Bridge: Existing Consuelo webhooks post to Discord channels
- /consuelo whisper @manager: Creates private thread with live audio link for coaching

## Technical Architecture

Discord/Slack -> Chat SDK Bot Service -> Consuelo API Gateway

What Chat SDK Provides:
- Unified API (write once, deploy to Discord + Slack)
- JSX Cards (rich interactive UIs with buttons, dropdowns, images)
- Event Handling (type-safe slash commands, button clicks, mentions)
- State Management (built-in Redis/in-memory adapters)

Auth Flow:
1. User runs /consuelo login
2. Bot DMs an OAuth link
3. User links Discord/Slack identity to Consuelo workspace
4. Tokens stored securely (same as existing CLI auth)

## Implementation Complexity Matrix

| Feature | Difficulty | Chat SDK Help | Notes |
|---------|-----------|---------------|-------|
| Slash commands | Easy | Yes | Native to chat SDK |
| Rich cards | Easy | Yes | JSX cards |
| Queue UI | Medium | Yes | Button handling |
| Call notifications | Medium | Yes | Event system |
| Thread persistence | Medium | Yes | Thread API |
| Voice channel status | Medium | Yes | Voice API |
| Discord voice bridge | Very Hard | No | WebRTC translation |
| Hybrid approach | Medium | Yes | Recommended |

## Why This Matters

For Reps:
- Never leave Discord/Slack (where they already coordinate)
- One interface for calls + team chat
- Less context switching = more calls made = more money
- Voice channel presence = social accountability

For Managers:
- See team activity in real-time without dashboard
- Jump into calls for coaching via Discord
- Celebrate wins in public channels for morale

For Consuelo:
- Platform expansion without building separate mobile apps
- Discord communities become organic acquisition channel
- Chat-native sales tools is a differentiator
- Network effects - teams invite other teams

## Next Steps

1. Spike: Set up basic Chat SDK bot with Discord adapter
2. Prototype: Port 3-5 most-used CLI commands (/me, /status, /queue start)
3. Test: Internal dogfooding with Consuelo team
4. Decide: Full voice bridge vs hybrid approach
5. Ship: Discord integration first, Slack second

Estimated Timeline:
- Phase 1 (Chat CLI): 2-3 weeks
- Phase 2 (Hybrid): 4-6 weeks
- Phase 3 (Advanced): 2-4 weeks
- Phase 2b (Voice Bridge): 6-12 weeks (if pursued)

## References

- Chat SDK: https://chat-sdk.dev/
- Vercel Announcement: https://vercel.com/changelog/chat-sdk
- Twilio Voice SDK: https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/sdks/javascript
- Discord Voice: https://discord.js.org/#/docs/discord.js/stable/class/VoiceConnection

---
